title: Time-Series Database (InfluxDB Cloud)
description: InfluxDB Cloud is a fully-managed serverless time-series database. Offers usage-based pricing, automatic scaling, and integrated visualization. Best for: cloud-native IoT applications, serverless architectures, rapid development without infrastructure management. Provides managed infrastructure with built-in monitoring and alerting.

provides.reasoning: InfluxDB Cloud is fundamentally a time-series database system designed for storing, querying, and managing time-stamped data with full database capabilities, not just storage.
InfluxDB Cloud provides automatic scaling as a core managed service feature, handling resource allocation based on workload demands without manual intervention.
InfluxDB Cloud includes integrated visualization tools and dashboards for time-series data exploration, allowing users to create charts directly within the service.
InfluxDB Cloud provides query APIs (Flux and InfluxQL) for data access, enabling external services and applications to interact with the database programmatically.
The pattern description explicitly mentions built-in monitoring as a key feature of the managed infrastructure, providing visibility into database and application metrics.
Built-in alerting is explicitly mentioned in the description and is a core feature for time-series monitoring use cases, enabling proactive notification of threshold breaches.

requires.reasoning: As a cloud-based service, InfluxDB Cloud requires internet connectivity for applications to access the database and its management interfaces.

provider.description: Cloud provider for managed InfluxDB service

retention_days.description: Data retention period in days (affects storage costs; shorter retention reduces costs)

billing_model.description: Billing model: usage-based for pay-as-you-go flexibility, annual-contract for discounted rates with commitment

query_language.description: Query language: Flux (recommended, more powerful with functional syntax) or InfluxQL (SQL-like, familiar but limited)

data_replication.description: Enable multi-region data replication for high durability and disaster recovery (increases costs)

write_precision.description: Timestamp precision: nanosecond (ns), microsecond (us), millisecond (ms), or second (s). Higher precision increases storage requirements.

service_auth_method.description: InfluxDB Cloud uses token-based authentication exclusively for API access.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: InfluxDB Cloud offers a generous free tier (5MB writes/5min, 300MB storage, 30-day retention) suitable for small IoT projects or development. Min $0 represents free tier usage. The $800 max represents a mid-to-large production deployment with ~100GB writes/month, ~500GB storage, unlimited retention, and higher query volumes. This aligns with their Usage-Based pricing tier ($0.002/MB write, $0.25/GB storage/month). Enterprise deployments with high cardinality data and extensive retention could exceed this, but $800 represents a typical production IoT/monitoring workload.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity adoption. While InfluxDB Cloud is serverless and eliminates infrastructure setup, teams need to learn InfluxQL/Flux query languages (different from SQL), understand time-series data modeling (tags vs fields, cardinality management), configure data retention policies, and integrate with existing applications. The learning curve for time-series-specific concepts (downsampling, continuous queries, bucketing) is significant. Estimated 40-60 hours for a mid-level team to properly implement, test, and optimize queries. Lower than self-hosted InfluxDB due to managed nature, but higher than traditional RDBMS due to specialized knowledge required.
provenance.licenseCost: InfluxDB Cloud operates on a pure usage-based SaaS model with no upfront license fees. No enterprise license required - all features scale with usage. Free tier available for development/testing. Costs are entirely consumption-based (pay-as-you-go for writes, storage, and queries). No commercial licenses, seat licenses, or annual contracts required for base service.

supports_nfr.reason: Managed serverless InfluxDB Cloud architecture supports up to 99.99% availability with multi-AZ deployment.
Time-series database queries achieve p95 latency floor of 5ms for optimized time-series data access patterns.
Complex aggregation queries and tail latency result in p99 floor of 20ms for time-series workloads.
Managed service with automated failover achieves RTO of 1-5 minutes through health monitoring and auto-recovery.
Auto-scaling cloud database handles up to 100k writes/sec (360M writes/hour) at peak throughput ceiling.
Time-series database optimized for append-heavy writes supports read query ceiling of 50k QPS with downsampling.
Time-series optimized ingestion pipeline supports write query ceiling of 100k QPS for high-volume IoT data.
Managed service provides comprehensive audit logging for data access, query execution, and configuration changes as optional feature.
InfluxDB Cloud supports tenant isolation through dedicated databases or schemas per organization with resource quotas.
Time-series database can store any data type including PII with appropriate encryption and access controls.
Cloud service provides GDPR compliance features including data residency, encryption, and right-to-deletion APIs.
Enterprise tier supports HIPAA compliance with BAA agreements, encryption at rest/transit, and audit logging.
Data access controls, retention policies, and deletion APIs support CCPA consumer privacy requirements.
Audit logging, access controls, and data retention policies support SOX compliance for financial time-series data.
Time-series writes are immediately queryable with read-your-writes consistency for real-time dashboards or eventual consistency for analytics.
InfluxDB Cloud implements strict durability with write-ahead logging and multi-AZ replication for committed writes.

supports_constraints.reason: InfluxDB Cloud SaaS runs on multiple cloud providers and is accessible from any cloud environment via API.
InfluxDB Cloud provides client libraries for all major programming languages via HTTP API and native SDKs.
Time-series database supports any platform architecture requiring time-series data storage and querying capabilities.
Pattern specifically provides time-series database capability and requires this feature flag to be active.
Pattern specifically requires influxdb-cloud as the database service
Database pattern is compatible with applications that use or don't use separate caching layers for query optimization.
Time-series database works with both synchronous and asynchronous data ingestion patterns via HTTP or streaming protocols.
Database can store time-series metrics from AI inference workloads or other application telemetry data.
Database supports both single-tenant and multi-tenant application architectures through organization and bucket isolation.
Time-series database supports both real-time ingestion and batch data loading workflows via bulk write APIs.
Database pattern is compatible with applications requiring or not requiring distributed transaction coordination.
Time-series database excels at real-time streaming data ingestion but also supports batch and periodic writes.
Time-series database is compatible with applications that may use separate vector databases for embedding search.
Time-series database can coexist with document stores in hybrid data architectures for different data access patterns.
Time-series database can complement key-value stores for different data access patterns in polyglot persistence.
Time-series database can coexist with graph databases in polyglot persistence architectures for relationship queries.
Time-series database optimized for append-heavy writes supports OLTP-style ingestion patterns and point queries.
Time-series database supports analytical queries over historical data with downsampling and aggregation functions.

warn_nfr.reason: InfluxDB Cloud has overhead (managed service, network latency, billing granularity). At <10 writes/sec, simpler storage (relational DB with timestamp column) may be more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: db-timeseries--influxdb-cloud: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (<10). Managed time-series DB overhead may not be justified; consider simpler storage.

warn_nfr.reason: At <1 write/sec (3600/hour), usage-based pricing and managed service overhead outweigh benefits. Time-series database features underutilized for low-volume periodic writes.
warn_nfr.message: db-timeseries--influxdb-cloud: peak jobs per hour is {actual} (<3600, or ~1/sec). Low write volume may not justify managed time-series DB costs.
